provenance.author: Gemini Code Assist
provenance.context: A consolidated knowledge base for Minecraft, created by merging all legacy .json data files into a single, structured semantic capsule.

intent.primary_goal: To provide a single, authoritative, and structured source of core Minecraft knowledge (biomes, entities, commands, etc.) for the CobbleWright agent, replacing the scattered legacy JSON files.

identity.role: A comprehensive, consolidated knowledge base about core Minecraft mechanics, items, and concepts.

plains.summary: A flat, grassy biome with rolling hills and few trees. Ideal for large, open-concept builds and farming.

forest.summary: A common biome with many oak and birch trees. Excellent for wood resources.

swamp.summary: A flat, wet biome with shallow pools, vines, and dark-colored water. Slimes and witches spawn here.
features: Ideal for stilt houses or witch huts

desert.summary: A barren biome of sand dunes, cacti, and dead bushes. Features unique structures like pyramids and wells.
features: Good source of sand and sandstone
Ideal for pyramids or adobe-style homes

taiga.summary: A cool forest of spruce trees, ferns, and berry bushes. Often has wolves and foxes.
features: Good for log cabins or rustic builds

gamemode.summary: Sets a player's game mode (e.g., creative, survival). Useful for switching to creative for large builds.

time.summary: Changes the world's game time. Useful for skipping the night.

weather.summary: Sets the weather. Useful for stopping rain.

fill.summary: Fills a region with a specific block. Excellent for clearing large areas or building walls quickly.

villager.summary: An intelligent mob that can be traded with for valuable items. Has distinct professions.

sheep.summary: A passive mob that can be sheared for wool, a key ingredient for beds.

sniffer.summary: An ancient creature that sniffs out rare decorative seeds (torchflower, pitcher).

enderman.summary: Becomes hostile if looked at directly. Teleports and drops Ender Pearls, which are vital for reaching The End.

iron_golem.summary: A massive defender of villages. Will attack if you harm villagers. Drops iron ingots.

creeper.summary: A silent, explosive mob that is a primary threat to players and their structures. Drops gunpowder.

breeze.summary: A mischievous mob from Trial Chambers that shoots wind charges, causing knockback and activating mechanisms.
breeze.special_notes: Its projectiles can be used to activate redstone components.

warden.summary: A blind and incredibly powerful mob found in the Deep Dark. Hunts using sound and vibrations. Extremely dangerous.

game_history.summary: Minecraft is a sandbox building and survival game created by Markus Persson, later developed by Mojang, first released publicly in 2009 and fully released as 1.0 in 2011. Its design history is centered on creative building, survival progression, redstone automation, exploration, and long-running Java Edition updates.
eras: Classic and Indev established freeform block-building, mining, and procedural worlds.
Alpha and Beta established survival loops, basic farming, the Nether, and the game's early identity.
Release 1.0 formalized enchanting, The End, and the modern progression structure.
The Redstone and technical era expanded automation, command systems, and contraptions.
Village, exploration, Nether, caves, and world-generation updates steadily deepened progression and biome variety.
The 1.21 generation emphasizes modern combat/set-piece design, newer blocks, and richer exploration while preserving sandbox creativity.
java_1_21_1_context: CobbleWright currently targets Minecraft Java Edition 1.21.1.
Advice should stay compatible with Java mechanics, blocks, mobs, commands, and progression for 1.21.1.
When uncertain about an exact block or mechanic, prefer conservative guidance rather than inventing features.

hopper_clock.summary: A silent, reliable timer that uses two hoppers and a comparator to create a repeating pulse. Excellent for timing farms or other machines.
hopper_clock.use_case: Creating timed pulses for automated systems.

auto_smelter.summary: An automatic furnace system that uses hoppers to feed fuel and items into a furnace, and collect the output. A foundational automation project.
auto_smelter.use_case: Automatically smelting ores or cooking food without player intervention.

small_hut.name: Small Hut
small_hut.description: A cozy 4x4 starter hut made of wood and cobblestone, perfect for a first night.

night_bunker.name: Night Bunker
night_bunker.description: A quick 3x3 dirt shelter with a doorway, built for fast nighttime cover.

watchtower.name: Simple Watchtower
watchtower.description: A 3x3 cobblestone watchtower to help you see approaching dangers.

rustic.summary: A cozy, natural style emphasizing wood and stone. Features simple shapes, exposed beams, and a connection to the outdoors.

modern.summary: A clean, minimalist style focused on geometric shapes, open spaces, and smooth textures.

medieval.summary: A defensive, grand style characterized by stone walls, towers, and decorative wood accents.

trust.note: This capsule is a high-confidence consolidation of previously separate, trusted knowledge files. It should be considered authoritative for core Minecraft game knowledge.
